title: Coding Basics
description: Software development from syntax to algorithms.

questions.text: What does HTML stand for?
In Python, how do you print 'Hello'?
Which company made the Java language?
What is CSS used for?
Which tag is for a paragraph?
In JavaScript, which keyword is used to declare a block-scoped variable?
What does the 'HTTP' acronym stand for?
Which data structure uses the LIFO (Last In, First Out) principle?
What is the default port for an HTTP server?
In Java, what is the parent class of all classes?
Which SQL clause is used to filter results?
What is the result of '2' + 2 in JavaScript?
Which of these is NOT a primitive data type in Java?
In Git, which command is used to download a repository from a remote source?
What does CSS stand for?
In Python, how do you start a for loop?
What is the time complexity of searching in a Hash Map (average case)?
Which HTML tag is used for the largest heading?
In C++, which operator is used for the 'address-of'?
What does JSON stand for?
Which command is used to list files in a directory in Linux?
In SQL, what does 'CRUD' stand for?
Which protocol is used for sending emails?
What is the binary representation of the decimal number 5?
In JavaScript, which function is used to parse a string into an integer?
Which CSS property is used to change the text color?
What is the primary purpose of a 'Docker' container?
In Java, which keyword is used to inherit a class?
Which symbol is used for comments in Python?
What does DOM stand for in web development?
Which of these is a NoSQL database?
In Git, which command stages changes for a commit?
What is the purpose of the 'alt' attribute in an tag?
Which programming language is known as the 'mother of all languages'?
In JavaScript, what is 'NaN'?
What is the worst-case time complexity of the QuickSort algorithm?
Which Gang of Four (GoF) design pattern is used to decouple an abstraction from its implementation so that the two can vary independently?
In Java, what is the key difference between '==' and the '.equals()' method when comparing two String objects?
options: '==' is only valid for primitive numeric types

questions.text: What is the term for a concurrency issue where two or more processes are unable to proceed because each is waiting for the other to release a resource?
In C#, what does the 'yield return' statement do inside an iterator method?
options: It returns one element at a time, pausing execution until the next iteration
It stops the loop and returns the entire collection immediately
It spawns a new thread to run the loop in the background
It throws an exception if the collection is empty

questions.text: Which protocol is typically used by Git for secure, passwordless authentication and data transfer?
What is the maximum number of nodes in a binary tree of height 'h' (where height of a single root node is 0)?
In networking, what does the 'CIDR' notation represent?
Which sorting algorithm is stable and guarantees a time complexity of O(n log n) in all cases (best, worst, average)?
What is the time complexity of prepending (inserting at the beginning) an element into a singly linked list with 'n' elements?
In Python, what is a decorator?
options: A function that takes another function as an argument and extends its behavior without modifying it
A class attribute used to design user interfaces
A mechanism to declare constant variables
A built-in GUI library in the standard library

questions.text: In Java, what is the primary purpose of the 'volatile' keyword when applied to a class field?
options: To guarantee that reads/writes to the variable are synchronized across all threads
To prevent the variable from being serialized during network transmission
To make the variable read-only and constant
To speed up calculation performance on multi-core CPUs

questions.text: Which HTTP status code is returned by a server when it encounters an unexpected condition that prevents it from fulfilling the request?
What is the primary architectural difference between a Process and a Thread in operating systems?
options: Threads share the memory space of their parent process, whereas processes have independent memory spaces
Processes are lightweight compared to threads
Threads cannot be run concurrently on multi-core systems

questions.text: In relational databases, which SQL operator is used to combine rows from two or more tables based on a related column?
In JavaScript, what is the result of evaluating '0.1 + 0.2 === 0.3' due to floating-point representation?
Which asymmetric encryption algorithm relies on the mathematical difficulty of factoring large prime numbers for its security?
In React, what is the primary purpose of the 'useEffect' hook?
options: To perform side effects in functional components

questions.text: What is a 'Race Condition' in concurrent programming?
options: A bug where the output of a program depends on the non-deterministic execution order of concurrent threads
An application crash when a thread takes too long to respond
A competition between processes to see which completes first
A network bottleneck due to high packet speeds

questions.text: Which data structure is most appropriate and efficient for implementing a multi-level 'Undo' history in an application?
On a standard 64-bit operating system, what is the typical memory size of an 'int' data type in C?
What does the 'S' stand for in the 'SOLID' object-oriented design principles?
In database systems, which transaction isolation level is the highest and completely prevents 'Dirty Reads', 'Non-Repeatable Reads', and 'Phantom Reads'?
What is the worst-case time complexity of searching for a value in a balanced Binary Search Tree (like an AVL tree) with 'n' nodes?
In Linux/UNIX administration, what signal is sent to a process when executing the 'kill -9' command?
What is the primary benefit of deploying a 'Reverse Proxy' server like Nginx in front of a web application?
options: All of these (Load balancing, SSL termination, and security caching)
Intelligent load balancing across backend nodes
Static content caching and security filtering

questions.text: Which data structure is typically used to implement a Breadth-First Search (BFS) algorithm on a graph?
In object-oriented programming, what is the 'Diamond Problem'?
options: An ambiguity that arises when a class inherits from two classes that both inherit from a single superclass
A memory leak caused by circular object references
An infinite recursion bug when calling overloaded constructors
A performance bottleneck in deep hierarchy trees

questions.text: What is the purpose of the 'finally' block in a try-catch-finally exception handling structure?
options: To execute cleanup code regardless of whether an exception was thrown or caught
To catch any remaining unhandled exceptions
To throw a new exception to the calling method

questions.text: Which layer of the OSI network model is responsible for logical addressing, routing, and packet forwarding?
